sources.notes: Official Educational Round 186 editorial page; used with the provided official solution code to confirm the average-capacity test and multinomial count.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Count valid ways to distribute tree decorations after normalizing every color/group to the same target amount.
annotation.constraints: The input sizes allow linear scans and factorial precomputation, so the hard part is deriving a closed combinatorial formula.
annotation.core_idea: Let the target amount be the average k. Each non-special group can keep at most k items; deficits are supplied by the special/root group. After filling every deficit, the remaining counts must be feasible: no non-special group may have leftover more than 1 and the root stock cannot become negative. If z non-special groups end at zero and the root has a0 remaining, the count reduces to arranging indistinguishable slots, giving z! * (n-z+a0)! / a0! modulo MOD.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after factorial precomputation.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Check average feasibility, greedily fill deficits from the root count, then compute the remaining arrangement count with factorials.

tags.evidence: The final answer is a factorial quotient counting arrangements of zero groups and remaining root items.
Feasibility is characterized by average divisibility, root stock not going negative, and every non-special leftover being at most one.
The deficits below the average are filled greedily from the root stock because only the total remaining root stock matters.
The solution is a counting formula after a feasibility reduction.
